brand.name: LeadStoke
brand.tagline: We Help You Build Your Customer List!

offer.what: Done-for-you and DIY lead generation programs for local small businesses: Yelp Help™ (review management), Review Tap™ (review generation), Birthday Marketing Formula™, Coupon Club™, email/SMS marketing, and digital display advertising
offer.coreOutcome: Build a owned buyer list that generates a steady stream of repeat customers without relying on expensive agencies or long-term contracts
offer.pricePoint: Unknown exact tiers; positioned as affordable for 'any budget'; no contracts, month-to-month
leadMagnets: Free trial on most programs (no upfront fee)

audience.icp: Local small business owners (restaurants, retail, personal services, wellness) who are frustrated with expensive marketing agencies and have no reliable system for growing a customer list
painPoints: Marketing feels complicated and out of reach
Burned by expensive agency retainers with no results
No owned customer list — dependent on walk-ins or word of mouth
Don't know how to get online reviews or leverage them

salesCycle.length: Days to a couple of weeks — founder-led consultative sale via phone or email
salesCycle.trial: Yes — free trial offered on most/all programs with no upfront cost
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Direct phone call or email with founder Dr. Jay Herrera (956-203-9533 / Jay@LeadStoke.com)
salesCycle.topObjection: Marketing is too complicated and I've already wasted money on agencies that didn't deliver

ahaMoment: First real lead, new customer visit, or online review generated through one of the programs — confirms the system works before any payment is required

retention.recurring: Yes — ongoing monthly programs (review management, birthday marketing, coupon club, SMS/email campaigns)
retention.expansion: Cross-sell additional LeadStoke programs (e.g., start with Review Tap, add Birthday Marketing Formula, then Coupon Club)
retention.churnRisk: Business owners who don't engage with the program, don't see quick early wins, or who feel they can't dedicate even minimal time to DFY follow-up

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — likely informal or absent; no structured activation sequence
Lead-Magnet / Free-Trial Nurture — prospects who start a trial need email support to convert to paid
Trial-to-Paid Conversion — critical gap given free-trial-first model
Booking / Consultation Reminders — no-show risk on initial calls with Jay
Value Newsletter — educational content to keep subscribers warm between programs
Renewal & Expansion — upsell cadence to move single-program clients to multi-program
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — lapsed trial users or cancelled clients
toneVoice: Warm, plain-spoken, founder-led. Empathetic to small business frustration with marketing. Anti-jargon, anti-corporate. Conversational and direct — feels like advice from a fellow business owner, not a pitch from an agency.
compliance: CAN-SPAM / TCPA (email + SMS marketing services). No healthcare, financial, or legal vertical constraints evident.
